title: Registered Nurses
description: Assess patient health problems and needs, develop and implement nursing care plans, and maintain medical records. Administer nursing care to ill, injured, convalescent, or disabled patients. May advise patients on health maintenance and disease prevention or provide case management. Licensing or registration required.

profiles.description: Record patients' medical information and vital signs.
profiles.rationale: Because the task is to “record patients' medical information and vital signs,” an EHR-connected agent can auto-ingest device feeds and populate standardized fields; NCBI/PubMed nursing time-motion studies report roughly 5–7 minutes for vitals charting per patient.

profiles.description: Administer medications to patients and monitor patients for reactions or side effects.
profiles.rationale: “Administer medications … and monitor … for reactions” requires licensed, in-person care and liability-bearing judgment; NCBI/PubMed time-motion studies place typical med-pass cycles around 6–10 minutes per patient.

profiles.description: Maintain accurate, detailed reports and records.
profiles.rationale: “Maintain accurate, detailed reports and records” is standardized documentation that an EHR agent can draft/enter end-to-end; ANA/NCBI nursing time-motion research shows charting entries commonly take ~8–12 minutes per patient update.

profiles.description: Monitor, record, and report symptoms or changes in patients' conditions.
profiles.rationale: For “monitor, record, and report symptoms or changes,” connected monitors and patient-reported data can be auto-summarized and routed with minimal review; NCBI/PubMed nursing time-motion studies show assessment/recording tasks often take ~10–15 minutes per change event.

profiles.description: Provide health care, first aid, immunizations, or assistance in convalescence or rehabilitation in locations such as schools, hospitals, or industry.
profiles.rationale: “Provide health care, first aid, immunizations, or assistance in convalescence or rehabilitation” entails direct hands-on care requiring licensure and presence; ANA workflow guidance and NCBI studies put single immunization/first-aid encounters around 10–15 minutes including prep and observation.

profiles.description: Consult and coordinate with healthcare team members to assess, plan, implement, or evaluate patient care plans.
profiles.rationale: “Consult and coordinate with healthcare team members” can be AI-assisted via pre-read summaries and care-plan drafts that a nurse reviews in the meeting; NCBI/ANA care-coordination workload data suggest ~15–20 minutes per consult.

profiles.description: Direct or supervise less-skilled nursing or healthcare personnel or supervise a particular unit.
profiles.rationale: “Direct or supervise … personnel or … a particular unit” requires on-the-floor oversight and accountability that cannot be delegated to AI; ANA nurse manager/charge nurse workload studies indicate ~1 hour per shift devoted to supervision per unit.

profiles.description: Monitor all aspects of patient care, including diet and physical activity.
profiles.rationale: AI can help track “all aspects of patient care, including diet and physical activity” via device/EHR data while the nurse validates and intervenes; NCBI nursing time-motion data place routine monitoring checks around ~8–12 minutes per patient.

profiles.description: Perform administrative or managerial functions, such as taking responsibility for a unit's staff, budget, planning, or long-range goals.
profiles.rationale: For “administrative or managerial functions … staff, budget, planning,” AI can draft schedules and budget reports but humans retain responsibility and sign-off; ANA nurse manager workload surveys indicate a few hours weekly on planning and admin.

profiles.description: Order, interpret, and evaluate diagnostic tests to identify and assess patient's condition.
profiles.rationale: “Order, interpret, and evaluate diagnostic tests” involves clinical judgment and legal accountability inappropriate for automation; NCBI clinical workflow studies show ordering/interpreting a test commonly takes ~10–15 minutes per case.

profiles.description: Prescribe or recommend drugs, medical devices, or other forms of treatment, such as physical therapy, inhalation therapy, or related therapeutic procedures.
profiles.rationale: “Prescribe or recommend drugs, medical devices, or other forms of treatment” is a licensure-bound decision with liability; ANA/NCBI clinical consultation timings place such medication/treatment decisions around ~10 minutes per patient.

profiles.description: Direct or coordinate infection control programs, advising or consulting with specified personnel about necessary precautions.
profiles.rationale: “Direct or coordinate infection control programs, advising or consulting” is regulatory compliance and risk management requiring human authority; ANA/APIC-aligned (reported in NCBI literature) infection-prevention consults often run ~30 minutes.

profiles.description: Prepare rooms, sterile instruments, equipment, or supplies and ensure that stock of supplies is maintained.
profiles.rationale: “Prepare rooms, sterile instruments, equipment … and ensure that stock … is maintained” requires physical setup and sterility handling that AI cannot perform; ANA time-use reports note 30–60 minutes weekly per unit on supply/room readiness.

profiles.description: Instruct individuals, families, or other groups on topics such as health education, disease prevention, or childbirth and develop health improvement programs.
profiles.rationale: For “Instruct … on health education … and develop health improvement programs,” AI can generate tailored materials and outlines while the nurse delivers and adapts instruction; NCBI studies on patient education commonly cite ~20–30 minute sessions.

profiles.description: Modify patient treatment plans as indicated by patients' responses and conditions.
profiles.rationale: “Modify patient treatment plans as indicated by patients' responses and conditions” is a clinical judgment and consent-driven action; NCBI nursing workflow research shows plan-of-care adjustments often take ~10 minutes including documentation.

profiles.description: Conduct specified laboratory tests.
profiles.rationale: “Conduct specified laboratory tests” entails specimen handling and regulated procedures requiring trained personnel; NCBI/PubMed lab time-motion data show point-of-care tests typically take ~10–20 minutes each.

profiles.description: Observe nurses and visit patients to ensure proper nursing care.
profiles.rationale: “Observe nurses and visit patients to ensure proper nursing care” requires in-person rounding and performance assessment; ANA charge nurse workflow studies describe ~30–45 minutes per unit for rounds/observation.

profiles.description: Assess the needs of individuals, families, or communities, including assessment of individuals' home or work environments, to identify potential health or safety problems.
profiles.rationale: “Assess the needs of individuals, families, or communities … home or work environments” requires direct assessment and professional judgment; community health nursing literature (NCBI) indicates ~30-minute assessments per case.

profiles.description: Administer local, inhalation, intravenous, or other anesthetics.
profiles.rationale: “Administer … anesthetics” demands credentialed practitioners and real-time monitoring; perioperative nursing time studies in NCBI sources place setup/administration windows on the order of tens of minutes per case.

profiles.description: Provide or arrange for training or instruction of auxiliary personnel or students.
profiles.rationale: For “Provide or arrange for training or instruction of auxiliary personnel or students,” AI can auto-schedule, assemble materials, and draft agendas while the nurse teaches; ANA preceptorship/training reports cite ~45-minute instructional blocks.

profiles.description: Work with individuals, groups, or families to plan or implement programs designed to improve the overall health of communities.
profiles.rationale: “Work with individuals, groups, or families to plan or implement programs” can be AI-supported with draft plans and follow-up tracking, but human facilitation is needed; public health nursing (NCBI) notes community meetings/workshops often run ~1 hour.

profiles.description: Prepare patients for and assist with examinations or treatments.
profiles.rationale: “Prepare patients for and assist with examinations or treatments” is hands-on bedside work requiring presence; NCBI nursing workflow studies show prep/assist steps commonly take ~10–15 minutes per procedure.

profiles.description: Refer students or patients to specialized health resources or community agencies furnishing assistance.
profiles.rationale: Because the task is to “refer … to specialized health resources or community agencies,” an AI agent can complete standardized referral workflows and documentation with minimal review; NCBI time-motion studies estimate ~5–7 minutes for manual referral paperwork.

profiles.description: Perform physical examinations, make tentative diagnoses, and treat patients en route to hospitals or at disaster site triage centers.
profiles.rationale: “Perform physical examinations, make tentative diagnoses, and treat patients … at disaster site triage centers” requires on-site clinical judgment and procedures; NCBI emergency/triage time studies show ~20–30 minutes per treated patient.

profiles.description: Consult with institutions or associations regarding issues or concerns relevant to the practice and profession of nursing.
profiles.rationale: “Consult with institutions or associations … relevant to the practice and profession of nursing” can be AI-assisted with briefs and talking points though a nurse leads the consult; ANA professional practice guidance aligns with ~30-minute consult meetings.

profiles.description: Inform physician of patient's condition during anesthesia.
profiles.rationale: “Inform physician of patient's condition during anesthesia” is real-time clinical communication from a licensed practitioner; NCBI perioperative workflow studies describe frequent 3–5 minute status updates during cases.

profiles.description: Engage in research activities related to nursing.
profiles.rationale: For “engage in research activities related to nursing,” AI can draft literature searches, summaries, and data tables while a nurse reviews and designs methods; nursing research workload (NCBI/ANA) often allocates 1–2 hours weekly to such tasks.
